goal: Make native Roblox water diving possible by ensuring the playable cell volume is real Terrain water and the spawn pad does not block descent.
root_cause: Native Roblox water can support diving, but the previous Studio probe found no Terrain water at the playable cell midpoint and no water at the nucleus area.
The player was likely interacting with a shallow or partial water patch rather than a deep Terrain water volume.
The invisible SpawnLocation was collidable, which could act like a hidden floor at the entry point.
changes: Added addNativeWaterVolume() to fill the whole playable swim bounds with Terrain water.
Set Terrain water color, transparency, reflectance, wave size, and wave speed for calmer underwater review.
Changed MainCellSwimSpawn.CanCollide to false so it cannot block diving.
Kept player movement mode as NativeWaterSwimming and did not reintroduce custom player LinearVelocity or VectorForce control.
validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() in Studio and confirmed full Terrain water samples around spawn and cell midpoint.
result: The cell bounds are now deep Terrain water, so native Roblox water controls should be able to dive by aiming/moving downward instead of being stuck on a shallow surface or hidden spawn floor.
